title: Understanding the Density Function
text: The density function is given by $$rho(x,y)=\rho_0 x y$$. This means the density varies with both $$x$$ and $$y$$ coordinates. How does this affect the calculation of the center of mass?

title: Formula for Center of Mass
text: The x-coordinate of the center of mass for a continuous body is given by the formula: $$x_{cm}$$ $$=$$ $$\frac{1}{M}$$ * $$\int_{0}^{a} x*rho(x,y) \,dxdy$$, where M is the total mass of the rectangle.

title: Calculate the Total Mass
text: To find the total mass M, integrate the density function over the entire area of the rectangle: M $$=$$ $$\int_{0}^{a} rho(x,y) \,dxdy$$.

title: Integrate the Density Function
text: Calculate the integral of the density function over the area of the rectangle to find the total mass M.

title: Substitute the Total Mass
text: Substitute the expression for the total mass M into the formula for $$x_{cm}$$.

title: Calculate $$x_{cm}$$
text: Using the formula for $$x_{cm}$$ and the expression for M, calculate the x-coordinate of the center of mass.
